item.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Our latest model, Claude Opus 4.8, is an upgrade to our Opus class of models, with stronger performance across coding, agentic tasks, and professional work, and the consistency to handle long-running work.

item.title: AMD Pulls a Bait-and-Switch on Linux Users with Vivado Licensing Changes
item.description: Tells Linux users to either pay up or get stuck on an aging, unsupported version forever.

item.title: Autism Works - Neurodiversity in the Workplace
item.description: Dave explains the benefits and responsibilities of autism in the workforce, with a focus on industrial scenarios.
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Beyond Benchmarks: Frontier LLM Disagreement on Fact-Checks
item.description: 67% of real-world claims expose disagreement among the five top frontier LLMs. Methodology, breakdowns, and data CSV.

item.title: Tuning in FM Radio on a 3D Printer Heatbed
item.description: Pooch from Repkord dropped by my studio while he was in St. Louis, and asked a simple question: Can a 3D printer's heatbed act as an antenna? A fair question, as many an antenna is embedded in a PCB these days... and the traces on a PCB heatbed like the one used in Prusa's Core One look kinda like an antenna, if you squint the right way. Really, anything (or anyone) can be an antenna, given enough power.

item.title: Hallucinate - Massively Multiplayer Online Rave
item.description: 78hair color

item.title: What Apple and Google are doing to your push notifications | Jacques Corby-Tuech
item.description: I wrote recently about what Google, Yahoo, Microsoft, and Apple are doing to your email: how four providers stopped being transport layers and turned into active intermediaries between brands and their customers, parsing, ranking, summarising, and increasingly answering on the recipient's behalf.

item.title: Can we have the day off?
item.description: So, apparently we are at the cusp of the entire world’s white collar workforce (and, by extension, much of the US workforce) undergoing a revolution in productivity. AI is the technology that is going to revolutionize the way we work, the way we interact with the world, the way we learn, the way we socialize, and all of this. This sounds great. Really, it does. Everything getting faster and easier would be an extraordinary boon to all of our lives.

item.title: Thranpages :: How Did I Do :: SimCity 3k in 4k
item.description: SimCity 3000 is the best SimCity. Its only problem is that it doesn't run well on contemporary systems. Not to worry, here's a complete guide to a perfect game of SimCity 3000 on modern Windows:...

item.title: I Vibe-Coded SwiperTab While Watching TV
item.description: Building SwiperTab (a browser extension) without manually writing a single line of code

item.title: Automating ADHD | 0xFF
item.description: Important

item.title: Search engine results are truly terrible (Maurycy's blog)
item.description: A few months ago, I had the displeasure of trying to use the modern web without an ad-blocker.

item.title: Linux was accidentally designed for agents
item.description: Beautiful, human-centered design isn’t appreciated by an agent.

item.title: I had Codex iterate on its own AGENTS.md 8 times and measured each version against real PRs. The best one still regressed on a clean holdout.
item.description: Codex optimized its own AGENTS.md against real Stet repo tasks. The best candidate improved the training slice, then regressed enough on a clean holdout that it was not safe to ship.

item.title: The Untold Story About W Social: Unconventional Beginnings, Strategic Pitches and Conflicting Signals
item.description: A deep dive into the origin story of W Social, an analysis of the strategic arguments they have been using to appeal to government officials, media companies and advertisers... and the discussion of conflicting signals they have been sending

item.title: Avoid Hasty Caching
item.description: Approach caching with caution.

item.title: Where are those goalposts? I’m sure I put them here somewhere
item.description: It’s been a few months since I personally ran out of places to move the AI goalposts to. And the news keeps coming. An internal model from OpenAI just refuted a long-standing mathematical conjecture. For the first time, AI has produced an indisputably novel and significant piece of mathematical knowledge. Several years ago, a lot of people would have said, yes, this is definitely intelligence. But now that it has happened, the goalposts need to get moved. Where to?

item.title: One million beings
item.description: It’s been a while.

item.title: How to Weigh a Cell
item.description: A single yeast cell weighs about one million times less than a grain of sand. How do we know this?

item.title: Why I Write | Hacky Experiments
item.description: I used to write for myself, but am doing it increasingly more for the machines.
item.author: (Bilal Tahir)

item.title: Your Skills Folder Is a Junk Drawer
item.description: Most skills shouldn't exist. The energy the community is pouring into building and collecting them is mostly wasted.

item.title: My Deaf Experience in Japan
item.description: Living with deafness in Japan for nearly 20 years — navigating work, communication, accessibility, and daily life as a deaf resident.

item.title: Time Machine Conversation
Improving AI labels for viewers and creators
item.description: We've heard consistently from our community that they value transparency when it comes to generative AI content. Two new updates will make this process much simpler and more intuitive for creators and viewers on YouTube.

item.title: Using My Fucking Brain
item.description: AI is great when it extends your brain. It gets dangerous when it quietly replaces the part that was supposed to think.

item.title: Show Me Less Output
item.description: The last time I saw someone showing off AI-generated reports, trying to present it as something insightful and helpful, I was really annoye...

item.title: Getting Claude to extract data from a 1997 football manager game
item.description: There's a football manager game I got for my birthday when I was nine years old: FIFA Soccer Manager 97. I spent countless hours playing it as a child, trying to win the league, the cup or just avoid getting the sack! Two years later I got a newer manager game with better graphics and more...

item.title: The Best Engineers Write Less Code
item.description: We are living in an age of mass denial. Some very smart people are telling us that we no longer need to write good software because LLMs can generate code now. But terrible software is still terrible software, whether it was written by Claude Code or by a human who did not know what they were doing. The computers running it are still real. Latency is still real. Complexity is still real. Maintenance is still real. All the old rules still apply. There is going to be an enormous amount of work fixing the slop now being pushed into production. But I digress.

item.title: An Engineering Leader’s Playbook for Delivering High Ambiguity and Rapidly Changing Product
item.description: A couple of weeks ago I gave an internal talk at IBM to the next batch of soon to be distinguished engineers. Distinguished engineer designation at IBM a highly coveted recognition. This blog is a brief summary of that hour long talk.

item.title: Running llama.cpp on a Mac Pro 6,1 with Dual FirePro D700s on Ubuntu
item.description: A D700-specific guide to running llama.cpp with Vulkan on the 2013 Mac Pro: dual 6 GB FirePro cards, Ubuntu, RADV, full GPU offload, cooling, and the traps that make old GCN hardware look slower than it is.
item.author: (Matthew Gribben)

item.title: Whistle
item.description:
I just realized people don’t whistle anymore.
I might even say they’ve forgotten about it. Even worse, if they hear someone whistling, they’ll probably b...

item.title: I think Anthropic and OpenAI have found product-market fit
item.description: Anthropic are strongly rumored to be about to have their first profitable quarter. Stories are circulating of companies surprised at how expensive their LLM bills are becoming from usage by …

item.title: DuckDuckGo's AI-free search saw nearly 28% more visits last week, but Google is still king
item.description: "People just want a choice."
item.author: (https://www.pcgamer.com/author/jess-kinghorn/)
